cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to figure out which statement is true. The first paragraph says that the British government is reviewing the use of lotteries to allocate school places because more than 20 percent of children are failing to get into their first-choice schools in parts of the country. This directly relates to option (B) as it says "Over one in five children fail to get into their first-choice schools in parts of Britain." One in five is 20 percent, so this seems correct. Let's check the other options to be sure. Option (A): The passage doesn't say the lottery system is certainly a good way.
